question: [File: 'File Name: importer/src/main/java/org/hiero/mirror/importer/reconciliation/BalanceReconciliationService.java -> Scope: Medium: Modification of transaction fees outside of design parameters'] [Function: reconcileCryptoTransfers()] Can an unprivileged external user submit a transaction whose fee transfer is recorded in the crypto_transfer table with an inflated amount, causing the CRYPTO_TRANSFER_QUERY sum to diverge from the account_balance snapshot and triggering a false FAILURE_CRYPTO_TRANSFERS status that permanently halts reconciliation under the FAIL remediation strategy?

[File: 'File Name: importer/src/main/java/org/hiero/mirror/importer/reconciliation/BalanceReconciliationService.java -> Scope: Medium: Modification of transaction fees outside of design parameters'] [Function: reconcileCryptoTransfers()] Can an unprivileged external user craft a transaction whose fee debit (negative amount) in crypto_transfer is recorded at a consensus_timestamp that falls exactly on the boundary between two balance snapshot windows, causing the CRYPTO_TRANSFER_QUERY range (consensus_timestamp > ? and consensus_timestamp <= ?) to exclude the fee debit while including the corresponding credit, producing a phantom balance discrepancy?

[File: 'File Name: importer/src/main/java/org/hiero/mirror/importer/reconciliation/BalanceReconciliationService.java -> Scope: Medium: Modification of transaction fees outside of design parameters'] [Function: getAccountBalances()] Can an unprivileged external user submit enough transactions with fee transfers such that the AtomicLong total in getAccountBalances() overflows Long.MAX_VALUE via total.addAndGet(balance), bypassing the FIFTY_BILLION_HBARS invariant check and causing reconciliation to silently accept a corrupted balance state?

[File: 'File Name: importer/src/main/java/org/hiero/mirror/importer/reconciliation/BalanceReconciliationService.java -> Scope: Medium: Modification of transaction fees outside of design parameters'] [Function: reconcileCryptoTransfers()] Can an unprivileged external user cause a fee transfer row to be inserted into crypto_transfer with errata='INSERT' (not 'DELETE'), so that the CRYPTO_TRANSFER_QUERY filter (errata is null or errata <> 'DELETE') includes the synthetic fee transfer in the balance sum, inflating the computed balance beyond the actual account_balance snapshot value?

[File: 'File Name: importer/src/main/java/org/hiero/mirror/importer/reconciliation/BalanceReconciliationService.java -> Scope: Medium: Modification of transaction fees outside of design parameters'] [Function: reconcileTransfers()] Can an unprivileged external user trigger repeated fee-amount mismatches across many balance file windows so that, under the ACCUMULATE remediation strategy, the error string accumulated via StringUtils.joinWith grows unboundedly in reconciliationJob.setError(), causing an out-of-memory condition or database column overflow that disrupts the reconciliation service?

[File: 'File Name: importer/src/main/java/org/hiero/mirror/importer/reconciliation/BalanceReconciliationService.java -> Scope: Medium: Modification of transaction fees outside of design parameters'] [Function: reconcileCryptoTransfers()] Can an unprivileged external user submit a transaction that generates a fee transfer to entity_id 98 (treasury) with an amount that, when summed via Math::addExact in transfersBalance.merge(), causes an ArithmeticException overflow, which is caught as FAILURE_UNKNOWN and permanently halts reconciliation without identifying the root cause?

[File: 'File Name: importer/src/main/java/org/hiero/mirror/importer/reconciliation/BalanceReconciliationService.java -> Scope: Medium: Modification of transaction fees outside of design parameters'] [Function: getTimestamp()] Can an unprivileged external user influence the timeOffset field of an AccountBalanceFile so that getTimestamp() returns a shifted value, causing the CRYPTO_TRANSFER_QUERY window to include or exclude specific fee transfers that should not be part of that reconciliation interval, silently skewing the fee balance comparison?

[File: 'File Name: importer/src/main/java/org/hiero/mirror/importer/reconciliation/BalanceReconciliationService.java -> Scope: Medium: Modification of transaction fees outside of design parameters'] [Function: reconcile()] Can an unprivileged external user cause a fee transfer to be recorded with a consensus_timestamp equal to the previous snapshot's timestamp (not strictly greater), so that the CRYPTO_TRANSFER_QUERY condition (consensus_timestamp > ?) excludes it from the current window, making the reconciliation pass even though the fee was not reflected in the balance snapshot?

[File: 'File Name: importer/src/main/java/org/hiero/mirror/importer/reconciliation/BalanceReconciliationService.java -> Scope: Medium: Modification of transaction fees outside of design parameters'] [Function: reconcileTokenTransfers()] Can an unprivileged external user submit a token transfer whose associated fee is recorded as a crypto_transfer but whose token_transfer entry is missing, causing reconcileTokenTransfers() to pass (no token discrepancy) while reconcileCryptoTransfers() fails, masking the fee manipulation behind a partial reconciliation success?

[File: 'File Name: importer/src/main/java/org/hiero/mirror/importer/reconciliation/BalanceReconciliationService.java -> Scope: Medium: Modification of transaction fees outside of design parameters'] [Function: getAccountBalances()] Can an unprivileged external user submit a transaction with a fee that causes the account_balance table to record a balance that does not equal FIFTY_BILLION_HBARS, triggering a ReconciliationException(FAILURE_FIFTY_BILLION) that halts all subsequent reconciliation and prevents detection of other fee manipulations?

[File: 'File Name: importer/src/main/java/org/hiero/mirror/importer/reconciliation/BalanceReconciliationService.java -> Scope: Medium: Modification of transaction fees outside of design parameters'] [Function: reconcileTransfers()] Can an unprivileged external user craft fee transfers such that Maps.difference(transfersBalance, currentBalances) produces a difference object whose toString() contains format specifiers (e.g., %s, %n), and when passed to String.format(failureStatus.getMessage(), ...) in the ACCUMULATE path, causes a format string injection that corrupts the error log or throws an exception?

[File: 'File Name: importer/src/main/java/org/hiero/mirror/importer/reconciliation/BalanceReconciliationService.java -> Scope: Medium: Modification of transaction fees outside of design parameters'] [Function: reconcile()] Can an unprivileged external user submit transactions with fee transfers timed such that the reconciliation job's consensusTimestamp is never advanced (because reconciliationJob.hasErrors() is always true), causing the job to perpetually re-process the same fee-discrepant window and never make forward progress?

[File: 'File Name: importer/src/main/java/org/hiero/mirror/importer/reconciliation/BalanceReconciliationService.java -> Scope: Medium: Modification of transaction fees outside of design parameters'] [Function: reconcileCryptoTransfers()] Can an unprivileged external user submit a transaction where the node fee transfer (positive amount to node account) is recorded in crypto_transfer but the corresponding payer debit (negative amount) is omitted or marked errata='DELETE', causing the CRYPTO_TRANSFER_QUERY to sum an unbalanced fee credit that inflates the computed balance above the snapshot?

[File: 'File Name: importer/src/main/java/org/hiero/mirror/importer/reconciliation/BalanceReconciliationService.java -> Scope: Medium: Modification of transaction fees outside of design parameters'] [Function: getLatestJob()] Can an unprivileged external user cause the reconciliation_job table to contain a stale consensusTimestamp (via a previously failed job) such that getLatestJob() resumes from a point before a known fee manipulation, causing the service to re-evaluate already-processed fee transfers and produce false positives or negatives?

[File: 'File Name: importer/src/main/java/org/hiero/mirror/importer/reconciliation/BalanceReconciliationService.java -> Scope: Medium: Modification of transaction fees outside of design parameters'] [Function: reconcile()] Can an unprivileged external user submit a high volume of transactions with small fee transfers such that the reconciliation loop processes more than 100 balance file windows before saving progress (reconciliationJob.getCount() % 100 == 0), and a crash mid-loop causes all fee discrepancies in those 100 windows to be lost and re-evaluated on restart?

[File: 'File Name: importer/src/main/java/org/hiero/mirror/importer/reconciliation/BalanceReconciliationService.java -> Scope: Medium: Modification of transaction fees outside of design parameters'] [Function: reconcileCryptoTransfers()] Can an unprivileged external user submit a transaction with a fee transfer where entity_id is set to a value that collides with an existing account in the transfersBalance map, causing transfersBalance.merge(accountId, balance, Math::addExact) to add the fee amount to the wrong account's running total, silently corrupting the balance comparison?

[File: 'File Name: importer/src/main/java/org/hiero/mirror/importer/reconciliation/BalanceReconciliationService.java -> Scope: Medium: Modification of transaction fees outside of design parameters'] [Function: reconcileTokenTransfers()] Can an unprivileged external user submit a token transfer with a fee denominated in a token (custom fee) that is recorded in token_transfer but whose TokenAccountId key collides with an existing entry in the tokenBalances map, causing tokenBalances.merge() to add the fee amount to the wrong token-account pair and corrupt token balance reconciliation?

[File: 'File Name: importer/src/main/java/org/hiero/mirror/importer/reconciliation/BalanceReconciliationService.java -> Scope: Medium: Modification of transaction fees outside of design parameters'] [Function: equals()] Can an unprivileged external user craft fee transfers that result in a transfersBalance map entry with value 0L for an account that is absent from currentBalances, causing equals() to return true (getOrDefault returns 0L) even though the account has a phantom fee entry, masking a fee discrepancy?

[File: 'File Name: importer/src/main/java/org/hiero/mirror/importer/reconciliation/BalanceReconciliationService.java -> Scope: Medium: Modification of transaction fees outside of design parameters'] [Function: reconcile()] Can an unprivileged external user submit transactions with fee transfers under the ACCUMULATE strategy such that previous.getBalances() (the stale balance map) is reused across iterations, causing fee discrepancies from earlier windows to compound with new fee transfers and produce a cumulative false mismatch that triggers FAILURE_CRYPTO_TRANSFERS?

[File: 'File Name: importer/src/main/java/org/hiero/mirror/importer/reconciliation/BalanceReconciliationService.java -> Scope: Medium: Modification of transaction fees outside of design parameters'] [Function: getNextBalanceSnapshot()] Can an unprivileged external user cause a gap in account_balance_file records such that findNextInRange(fromTimestamp, toTimestamp) skips a balance file containing a fee manipulation, allowing the fee discrepancy to be silently absorbed into the next reconciliation window without detection?

[File: 'File Name: importer/src/main/java/org/hiero/mirror/importer/reconciliation/BalanceReconciliationService.java -> Scope: Medium: Modification of transaction fees outside of design parameters'] [Function: reconcileCryptoTransfers()] Can an unprivileged external user submit a transaction with a fee transfer at a consensus_timestamp that equals current.getTimestamp() (the upper bound of the CRYPTO_TRANSFER_QUERY range), ensuring the fee is included in the sum, but then submit a corrective transfer at consensus_timestamp = current.getTimestamp() + 1 that falls outside the window, causing a permanent balance discrepancy?

[File: 'File Name: importer/src/main/java/org/hiero/mirror/importer/reconciliation/BalanceReconciliationService.java -> Scope: Medium: Modification of transaction fees outside of design parameters'] [Function: reconcileTransfers()] Can an unprivileged external user cause the FAIL remediation strategy to throw a ReconciliationException for a fee discrepancy, which is caught in reconcile()'s catch block and sets the job status to FAILURE_CRYPTO_TRANSFERS, permanently blocking reconciliation and hiding subsequent fee manipulations from ever being detected?

[File: 'File Name: importer/src/main/java/org/hiero/mirror/importer/reconciliation/BalanceReconciliationService.java -> Scope: Medium: Modification of transaction fees outside of design parameters'] [Function: getAccountBalances()] Can an unprivileged external user submit transactions with fee transfers that cause the account_balance table to record a balance of exactly FIFTY_BILLION_HBARS for the total, but with individual account balances that are incorrect (e.g., fee credited to wrong account), causing getAccountBalances() to pass the total check while the per-account fee distribution is silently wrong?

[File: 'File Name: importer/src/main/java/org/hiero/mirror/importer/reconciliation/BalanceReconciliationService.java -> Scope: Medium: Modification of transaction fees outside of design parameters'] [Function: reconcileCryptoTransfers()] Can an unprivileged external user submit a transaction with a fee transfer where the amount is Long.MIN_VALUE, causing Math::addExact in transfersBalance.merge() to throw ArithmeticException when added to an existing balance, which propagates as FAILURE_UNKNOWN and halts reconciliation?

[File: 'File Name: importer/src/main/java/org/hiero/mirror/importer/reconciliation/BalanceReconciliationService.java -> Scope: Medium: Modification of transaction fees outside of design parameters'] [Function: reconcile()] Can an unprivileged external user submit transactions with fee transfers that cause reconcileCryptoTransfers() to fail but reconcileTokenTransfers() to succeed, so that under the ACCUMULATE strategy the job status is set to FAILURE_CRYPTO_TRANSFERS but processing continues, allowing subsequent fee manipulations to be evaluated against a corrupted running balance?
